RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' motion to strike and/or tax costs is continued to June 1, 2017. The ruling on the Motion for reconsideration was vacated by the court and will also be heard on June 1, 2017. Opposition to the motion to tax cost and evidence as to Motion for Reconsideration to be filed by May 25, 2017; Reply for both motions to be filed by May 30, 2017. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion For Reconsideration And Review
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GUY CALLADINE, TEL. NO. 1-415-391-3911 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' motion for reconsideration of the March 13, 2017 order granting defendants' motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is denied. Ms. Karnazes has not identified any new facts, law or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the March 13 order. Ms. Karnazes' assertions that the March 13 order was motivated in whole or in part by or was the result of disability discrimination are unfounded. Nor do any of the disability accommodation statutes or judicial ethics rules warrant granting this motion. Notwithstanding that Ms. Karnazes has not shown any entitlement to reconsideration per CCP 1008, the court has, on its own motion, made another full review of the arguments and evidence on defendants' motion to dismiss and, based on that review, believes there is no basis to vacate or make any change to the March 13 order. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
